California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 22, 1988


William L. Corry
P.O. Box 795
Running Springs, CA  92382

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-113

Dear Mr. Corry:

You have asked for advice concerning the duties of the
Arrowbear Park County Water District Board of Directors under
the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform
Act.1/

The Commission's Legal Division does provide written advice
to persons with questions about their duties under the
Political Reform Act. (Section 83114(b).) We do not provide
advice to third parties about another person's duties unless
the third party has been authorized to request advice by the
person whose duties are in question. (Regulation
18329(b)(8)(B).) Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the
question you have raised. However, if you believe that the
Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement
Division. That division can be reached at (916) 322-6441.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code
of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code
of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

FPPC

William L Corry
P.O. 795
Running Springs, CA 92382

MAR 11 1988

California Fair Political Practices Commission,
Elaine M. Baffin
General Counsel
428 J Street, Suite 800
PO Box 207
Sacramento, CA 95804-0207

Dear Ms Baffin,

I want to thank you for your consideration and
advice on my request of Jan 26, 1988 your file I-88-025.
Enclosed for copies of minutes for Atascadero Park
County Water District meetings for Dec 1987 and Jan 1988.
I've underlined the portions where as I lay concerns.
In December we (76 public) were told that an attorney (a close
friend of director Williams) would sit on the committee
meeting, which would select the attorneys interested
in representing the District. He, the attorney, would do
this without charge on a friendship basis.

In the next item on the agenda Director Williams
made a plan for help in securing financing for the water
treatment plant. He would like the form of H.B. Capitol
Resources L.P. (who he deals with in his work with the County of
San Bernardino.) There were no other forms considered
and the financial arrangements had already been discussed
and set. The motion for hiring this form was made by
Director Williams. It passed.

at the next on acting Swisler Wellamme was elected
bad President of the Board. He did cause the for to be payed to H O
Capital Resources. Later in the meeting the attorney brought
up a point that would have in fact caused the fee not to be
paid at the time.
Later in the meeting we found that in reality, H B
Capital Resources Ltd and Steve Hammill are one and the
same. Did President Wellamme lend his friend $1000, and
depending of funding maybe as much as $5000? Why didn't
he make clear to the public and rest of the Board who
H.C. really was? It seems he [illegible] this paid from us with
maybe the ex captain of Swisler Acme B games.
Actions like this are carried on, on a regular basis
with the board, as I have pointed out before. Does
the carry over into the County of San Bernardino work
that President Wellamme does? It certainly was not right
and it does question his integrity.
I appreciate any help you can give me on this

Yours Truly
William ZLSing

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

January 26, 1988

William L. Corry
P.O. Box 795
Running Springs, CA 92382

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-88-035

Dear Mr. Corry:

We have received your letter regarding the Arrowbear Park
County Water District Board of Directors placing a tax override
issue on the November ballot without the input from property
owners.

The Fair Political Practices Commission has authority to
interpret and enforce the Political Reform Act. The
Political Reform Act was established for several purposes, some
of which are disclosure of sources of campaign contributions,
disclosure of assets and income of public officials,
disqualification of public officials from decisions which could
affect their private financial interests, and regulation of the
activities of lobbyists to prevent improper influences on
public officials.

The question you pose in your letter is not covered by the
Political Reform Act; therefore, we cannot advise you on this
matter. In reviewing the material you submitted, it appears
that you may be concerned about possible violations of the
Brown Act. It would be more appropriate for you to direct your
questions to your county district attorney.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Jeanette Turvill
Legal Assistant

JET:jaj

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Jan. 9, 1988
Arrowbear Lake, Hi L.S. P.O
Ca. 92382

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street Ste. 800
Sacramento,
Ca. 95814

Dear Sirs:

I am writing this letter in the hope of getting information on how to
correct a problem that I see in this area. It concerns taxation without
representation.

The area is a community, Arrowbear Lake, Ca. It consists of approximately
900 homes. The Arrowbear Park County Water District is a self governing
Special District with towers of water,sewer,and fire. It has a five man
Board of Directors, elected by the registered voters of the District.
There are about 240 registered voters, declared primary homes. This
leaves about 750 homes with no vote on issues of their taxes or water &
sewer rates.

I am enclosing copies of the minutes of the Districts (#1) May, 1987 and
(#2) June, 1987 meetings. The minutes will show the Board of Directors
did authorize a tax over-ride to be put on the November ballot and did
not notify all taxpayer - property owners in time for their input or
desires of need for same. I have highlighted the items of my concern
which mainly had to do with notification of all taxpayers. However, with
the response that the Committee for Fair Taxation received I feel the
possibility of a vote for all taxpayers in this type of District should
be pursued. To show you the tyre of response see the enclosed newspaper
clippings.

Any advice or help you can give me in regard to this matter will be
appreciated. This Board has been notorious from it.s beginning for
playing the surprise. They have telephone cr one on one conversations
reaching a concensus and then spring it at a Public meeting and not have
much in the way of discussion. To point this out I am enclosing Copies
of the March 14,1986 (#3) meeting Which I think is self explanetory

I thank you for whatever help or advice you can give me.

Yours truly,

ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
Regular Meeting
December 11, 1987

The regular meeting of the Board of Directors of the Arrowbear Park County
Water District was held December 11, 1987 at the Water Office, 2365 Fir
Drive Arrowbear Lake, California. The President declared the meeting open
at the hour of 7:30 p.m.

Directors in attendance were, Arne Bjaanes, John Medearis, Max McIlravy,
Marley Ray and Baxter Williams. Others in attendance were, Manager Wymer,
Richard Strong, Candis Gregory and John Timmer from CDF, Bill Corry, Sheila
Wymer, Bea Medearis, Mr. & Mrs. Norm Taylor, Mrs. Briske, Mary Ray, Edith
Corry, Mr. Van Berkelaar and Joan Mosley Bolas.

The Consent Agenda was presented for approval. Director Medearis moved to
accept the Consent Agenda, seconded by Director Williams.

Director Williams remarked that in the regular meeting minutes, paragraph
2 they should state President Bjaanes, not Director Williams made the
statement that the water budget should be included. Also in the same
paragraph the reason Sun Construction's bill was deferred was due to the
job not being accepted by the County.

Director Williams asked if the vacation and sick reports reflected the
usage of old time accumulation? This reply was that the past accumulation
is being deducted first. Manager Wymer also informed the Board that the
County has written off the job by Sun Construction. At this time President
Bjaanes called for a vote on the Consent Agenda. The motion carried,
Director McIlravy abstained.

Manager Wymer broached the subject of insurance for the district. Special
Districts Insurance Authority, without errors and omissions or comp. and
collision is $33,663.00. Errors and Omissions insurance for eight months
or until July 31st would be $2,000.00. The first year buy-in is 15% of the
yearly rate, or $5,050.00. We are still waiting on a price of collision
and comp.

The yearly rate from Kellogg and Moreland with Errors & Omissions and Comp.
& Collision is $25,914.00. The yearly rate from SDIA, without buy-in and
without collision and comp is 41% higher. There is no information on
umbrella coverage for the district, Kelloggs & Moreland are still looking.
Chief Taylor interjected that he is checking on insurance strictly for fire
departments. President Bjaanes stated that his feeling on the insurance
situation at this time is that of going with Kellogg & Moreland, and
starting a more thorough investigation for insurance ahead of time next
year.

Manager Wymer informed the Board that LAFC won't allow a parcel to be
split. We need to decide whether to drop that portion is in Running Springs
Deep Creek or include the entire property. However, the portion in
question is in Running Springs sphere of influence. After Discussion, the
consensus of the Board was to drop that portion knows as Deep Creek and

proceed with the annexation. Manager Wymer also informed the Board that
there could be some problems with the annexation. Green Valley Lake
Sewing district has some bonds against some of those parcels we are
proposing to annex. At this time Manager Wymer does not know how this will
affect annexation. It does know this they are charging for sewer service.
This could be worked out with a contract between the two districts.

President Bjaanes stated that LAFC is holding a hearing on January 20, 1988
in connection with the annexation; they will not oppose the annexation.
President Bjaanes interjected that Green Valley Lake is competing on the
annexation. Manager Wymer interjected that they meet our line, this is the
only information on this to date.

Manager Wymer broached the subject of which bills will be paid prior to the
accounts payable at the regular board meeting. After discussion this
matter was tabled to the January meeting.

Manager Wymer opened the subject of paving of the parking lot. A bid from
P & R Construction in the amount of $5,700.00 for a cement drive was has
been received. After discussion, Director Nedesals moved that we proceed
with the asphalt drive way. The motion died for lack of a second.

During further discussion, Director McIlravy moved to expend the funds, and
approve the contract submitted by P & R Construction, seconded by Director
Williams. Motion carried by a unanimous vote.

Manager Wymer informed the Board that he had received a letter from the
Department of Health Services informing us that we must include a
"statement with our monthly mailing, "Special Notice to all Employees,
Landlords, and Schools. Manager Wymer also informed the Board the letter
also informed us that continuous reliable chlorination will be required.

Manager Wymer went over the monthly report from John Egan with the Board.
Director McIlravy questioned item 2 on the list of permits and reviews
required for the treatment facility. Manager Wymer replied that the County
has not decided that the regenerate will be classified as a hazardous
waste, and that storage in accordance with the county ordinance will be
required. Manager Wymer believes that Mr. Egan was planning on meeting all
the requirements with the use of the double walled tank. At this point, we
still have design leeway.

Mr. Strong informed the Board that the language for the bond assignment has
been completed. The owners of District No. 5 have been contacted and and a
request made for bond power. Next month, a agenda item should be for the
signing of the bonds.

Mr. Strong informed the Board that he had gone over the contract with CDF,
and found no problems with it.

Chief Taylor informed the Board that there had been a total of 13 calls for
the month of November. 6 medical aids, 1 structure fire, 1 vehicle fire, 4
public services and 1 illegal fire.

Chief Taylor broached the subject of the contract with CDF for dispatching.

Lake Arrowhead wants $2,164.00 for next years service. CDF will only charge us for dispatch calls. If there would be a savings of $380.81 with CDF this year. CDF offers a 800 number in lieu of the 911, this would save us approximately $1,500.00 in phone bills. Chief Taylor pointed out that the same frequency would be dispatching the ambulance and fire, somewhere down the line we could have problems being dispatched by two difference agencies. Chief Taylor remarked that he had no problems with Lake Arrowhead, but felt that down the line there could be. Chief Taylor recommended that we go with CDF.

After discussion, Director McIlravy moved to accept the contract with CDF, effective March 1, 1988. Seconded by Director Ray. Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

Chief Taylor interjected that due to the rise in workman's comp insurance, the category of insurance will be over budget down the line. President Bjaanes referred this to the finance committee.

President Bjaanes informed the Board that the committee on the attorney had met. There will be a meeting in the board room December 17, 1987 for further interviews and a selection of three for presentation to the Board for the final selection of one.

Mary Ray asked, why a committee for the selection, should not the entire Board hear what these men have to say at the interview?

President Bjaanes replied that the last time a selection for the attorney took place, all the board sat in on the interviews, very few questions were asked, and it was very time consuming. This time there will be a attorney present who has no working knowledge of any of the candidates, he will be a part of the interviewing process. Director Ray asked who this was. President Bjaanes replied, Mr. Steve Hamill, he is doing this at no charge, on a friendship basis.

Director Williams, chairman of the finance committee reported on financing and low cost loans and possible grants have been looked for, however, none have been forth coming. Private lenders have been pursued, there should be no problem there. Some form of tax exempt financing is very likely. Director Williams suggested expending funds and retaining a financial consultant to procure funding for us. H.B. Capital retaining a financial consultant to procure funding for us. H.B. Capital Resources, who are in the Bay Area, will do this for a quarter of resources, who are in the Bay Area, will do this for a quarter of the local loan. The district would be under no obligation to accept any proposal that the company would come up with. The only out of pocket funds would be the initial $1,000.00 if the with. The only out of pocket funds would be the initial $1,000.00 if the district could reduce the size of bond on by 28, this would be worth the district could reduce the size of bond on by 28, this would be worth the cost on consultant. President Bjaanes remarked that we need to have a cost on consultant. President Bjaanes remarked that we need to have a financial package in place soon, the construction start is scheduled for early spring. After discussion,

Director Williams moved to seek the assistance of a financial consultant, seconded by Director Medearis. Motion carried by a unanimous vote.

Director Williams mentioned that several months ago we made a offer on the park, what has happened with that? President Bjaanes replied that he had

talked with Les Kole, this has been put on hold till the new board members
of the parks and recreation district can familiarize themselves with this.
No proposal has been made at this time to Don Bosco.

President Bjaanes remarked that the two managers should again start to look
at management by objectives.

There being no further business, the President adjourned the meeting at the
hour of 9:25 p.m.

The next regular meeting will be held January 15, 1988.

APPROVED:

_________________________Secretary

_____________________________
Arne Bjaanes, President

ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT
Regular Meeting
January 22, 1983

The regular meeting of the Board of Directors of the Arrowbear Park County
Water District was held January 22, 1983 at the Water Office, 2345 Fir
Drive Arrowbear Lake, California. The President declared the meeting open
at the hour of 7:30 p.m.

Directors in attendance were, Arna Bjaanes, John Madearis Max McElravy, W.
Ray and Baxter Williams. Others in attendance were, Manager Wymer, John
Rican, Richard Strong, Richard Anderson, Bill Corey, Stella Wymer, Norm
Taylor, Mr. Van Berckelsler, Mrs. Briski, and Pat Doramore.

At this time President Bjaanes turned the meeting over to legal counsel for
that portion of the meeting relating to annual election of officers. Mr.
Strong opened the floor for nominations for President. At this time
President Bjaanes stated that while his term as President had been at
times fun, and times difficult and at all times challenging, it is time to
step down. At this time Director Bjaanes nominated Director Williams.
Director Ray nominated Director Madearis. Director Madearis declined the
nomination. Nominations were closed, a vote was called. Directors Bjaanes
Madearis, Ray and McElravy; Ayes, Director Williams abstained. Director
Williams is the new President of the Board of Directors.

Mr. Strong opened the floor to nominations for the position of Vice
President. Director McElravy nominated Director Bjaanes. There being no
other nominations, the nominations were closed and a vote called.
Directors Madearis Ray, McElravy and Williams Ayes; Director Bjaanes
abstained. The new Vice President of the Board of Directors is Arne
Bjaanes.

At this time Mr. Strong turned the meeting over to President Williams.
President Williams remarked that this position was not one that he actively
pursued, largely because he had been satisfied with the Boards leadership
to this point.

Director Bjaanes moved to re-affirm the position of secretary-treasurer for
the coming year. Seconded by Director Madearis. Motion carried by a
unanimous vote of the Board.

Per instructions of the auditor, the Board will re-establish its payment
policy for the coming year. The policy established is $100.00 per meeting
attended, for each director up to a maximum of 6 meetings per month; with
the provision that special meetings and continued meetings as compared
with the provision that they exceed 1 hour in length. Motion by Director
Bjaanes that this policy be re-established, seconded by Director Williams.
Motion carried by a unanimous vote of the Board.

The Consent Agenda was presented for approval. Director Ray moved to
accept the Consent Agenda. Seconded by Director Madearis.

Director Williams asked if the bill to HB Capital Resources, Lts. in the

amount of $1,000.00 was paid? The reply was no, they have not submitted a
bill. This was added to the list of accounts payable. Director Williams
also asked if the back vacation and sick time was to be used first and
very was yes. At this time a vote was called for. The motion carried,
Director McIlvarry abstained.

Manager Wymer informed the Board that he, Norm and Director Bjaanes had
attended a meeting with LAFC January 20, 1988. There was no opposition to
the annexation, it went through with no problem. We will have to refil
boundary maps with the state. Director Bjaanes interjected that the
District will have to hold a public hearing on the annexation as part of
the final action. We will need approximately $900.00 from CSA 38 in
revenue, this will go to the fire department.

Manager Wymer introduced Resolution 88-1-22, A RESOLUTION OF THE BOARD OF
DIRECTORS OF THE ARROWBEAR PARK COUNTY WATER DISTRICT ESTABLISHING THE
MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY OF THE GENERAL MANAGER TO EXPEND FUNDS. President
Williams remarked that this is in part what we asked for. When can we
for something more definite of what bills get approved (a list of bills)".
Director McIlvarry felt that this should be something from the attorney, it
should be checked out with a Government Code. On Water District it should be
spelled out there what the Managers limitations are. President Bjaanes
stated that he was looking for a set of categories spelling out what bills
would be paid prior to board approval, contract items also need to be
specified. After discussion, this item was tabled to the February meeting
the Manager to come back with recommended types of payments, the Resolution
to be reviewed by Council before it comes back to the Board.

Manager Wymer informed the Board that CBDG Funds have a deadline of
February 24, 1988 for filing. We have the forms, we need to know what the
board wishes to apply for. This is a three year program, they carried our
previous application forward. It would by the Managers recommendation that
the District re-file for the same type thing we had before. 1) The sewer
dept. applied for a building and generator system for the lift station. 2)
the fire department applied for a fire station. Director Ray asked if we
could apply for replacement of any of the two inch lines? Manager Wymer
replied not under this type of funding. During discussion the possibility
of applying for funding with regard to uranium removal or the chlorination
of the treatment plant were possibilities. However the Manager stated that
the priority was the building and generator for the lift station. The
dollar figure that was applied for was $57,000.00, the fire department put
in for $150,000.00. After further discussion it was the consensus of the
Board that the three previous projects would be resubmitted.

Manager Wymer broached the subject of some type of uniform or
identification for the servicemen. One of the servicemen had gone out on a
night call looking for a leak, and he was challenged by a property owner.
After discussion Manager Wymer was instructed to come back to the February
meeting with prices on vests, uniforms, etc. Identification badges will be
purchased for the servicemen.

At this time Mr. Richard Anderson presented the assessment study for
approval. Manager Wymer asked if Mr. Anderson wanted a separate budget for
the assessment account, the reply was yes. Director Bjaanes remarked that

the Fund Accounts are a budget process. Mr. Anderson replied that this is
fine, they set out separate and distinct, however, they should be budgeted
separately.

Director Bjaanes stated that at the finances meeting, Mr. Anderson had
recommended that the computer be used for all the accounting procedures,
and indicated a need for cost centers. Director Bjaanes remarked that this
issue should be referred to the Manager, and that this should be brought
back to the Board with costs of the change over to a cost center process
including training, etc. We need this information in place for the start
of the next fiscal year. Manager Wymer replied that he had no problem in
doing this, however, he would have to rely on Mr. Anderson expertise when
it came time to determine the ratio and cost benefits of cost centers.

Director Bjaanes moved to adopt the recommendations made by Mr. Anderson.
With regard to the assessment funds, these to be incorporated into the
procedures followed in the budgeting process. Seconded by Director Ray.
During discussion it was pointed out that if you go ahead to pass the
study, they you should pass it in its entirety. If item "D" is to be left
out, the motion should so stipulate. Director Bjaanes amended his motion
to accept the report previously submitted and the recommendations presented
in the letter of October 27, 1987. President Williams asked if this was
acceptable to the seconder of the motion? Director Ray replied no, he has
not had a chance to study the report. Director Medearis seconded the
ammended motion. The motion carried, Director Ray abstained.

Part II of Mr. Anderson's report referred to the general accounting
procedure for the district, internal controls and a series of
recommendations pertaining thereto. "In order to gain better control over
the budget process and preparation of interim financial statements, we
recommend that the accounting system be revised. 1) Cost Centers be
established in the water and sewer departments. 2) More use of the
computer. 3) Adjustments should be made in the reports of major unpaid
items of receivables and payables. 4) Items used in the budgeting process
be the same as those used in the preparation of the financial statements.
These are the four basic areas that we feel you need to do something with.
The computer and buying some programs to put your basic records on will
help alot once you get them established with the training, etc. It is not
easy, but we feel it will help alot."

President Williams asked what degree of revisions are going to be
necessary, to what extent procedures used in prior accounting methods are
going to be disrupted, and what costs will we incur in doing this? The
finance committee met briefly, knowing that no action could be taken
tonight, but desiring that some direction could be taken. The
Finance Committee recommend that the Manager pursue alternative solutions
to the matter, addressing the recommendations made by the accountant, and
come back to the Board within 30 to 60 days with a recommendations and a
analysis as to the benefits and costs.

Director Ray remarked that he was confused when he read the front page,
what do you mean, when it is finished, you stated you don't have a legal
opinion? Mr. Anderson replied that we have to be consistent in order to
determine what kind of auditing procedure you require; but that doesn't

mean that we are going to give you a opinion that your system is fine, is
operating the way it should, or that there aren't limitations to it,
because there are all kinds of qualifications. Realistically employees can
circumvent the system. You can put a check on it, there is a draft
on you. What this does is say we have looked at your system but we are not
going to give you a opinion on the function of the controls. Does that
answer your question? Director Ray stated no, it just looked at the system,
and gave us your recommendations, to me the system is either all right or
it isn't. Mr. Anderson replied, let me put it this way, there might be
things out there that I don't know about. We may revisit, there's fine,
but I have never seen a report made on anything that came up with a
conclusion that we don't have a opinion on it. President Williams stated
that the language appears on nearly every audit I have ever seen with the
possible exception of what I here referred to as a full audit. Mr.
Anderson remarked that the purpose of this letter is to point out to the
Board the areas that we feel could result in some serious problems. That is the purpose of this audit. President Williams
interjected resulting in some minor ones, and until steps are taken leave
the door open for some major ones.

Mr. Egan went over his monthly report with the Board, and gave a list of
the required permits and licenses as requested by the Board at the December
meeting. A letter was sent to Mr. John Hickman of State Health Department
in Sacramento on plant design and operations. About 10 days ago we
received a call from Mr. Hickman giving a favorable review of the project.
No permit will be required for storage of the uranium. Mr. Egan feels that
we have cleared all concerns on the design of the plant.

Mr. Egan informed the Board that he had discussed uranium removal and
disposal with Mr. Annamalai of IEC Corporation in Texas. It is possible
that the firm would be interested in using the uranium contained in the
regenerant.

Mr. Egan discussed the design of the plant, and suggested that 3 contracts
be let. 1) plant only, 2) storage tank, 3) general contractor to build the
plant and install the equipment, etc.. The District is to install the pipe
line in the ground. The three contracts and 4 ditto will result in a
cheaper project. Mr. Egan stated that in about a month he will be ready to
seek authorization of the health department on the whole project.
President Williams asked when we would be able to go to bid? Mr. Egan
stated that he felt we would be ready to seek authorization in March.
President Williams asked if all three bids would go out at the same time?
Mr. Egan replied yes.

Mr. Strong informed the Board that he had reviewed the contract with HB
Capital Resources, Ltd., and at this time I am not telling you that you should
not approve the contract, I am simply pointing out that HB Capital
Resources should not be paid a fee until they provide us with information
verifying financing sources we have already identified. I understand that
this is already being worked out."

Mr. Strong pointed out that we have received the proper bond powers and
language, the bonds can now be signed and transferred to General Electric
Corporation.

President Williams interjected that he had discussed with Mr. Hamil of us
Capital Resources the fact that the District had already made contacts with
regard to the pursuit of funds in various programs and learned nothing about
sharing funds from these sources. Mr. Strong suggested that said
clarification be delt with in the form of a letter from the president of
the Board.

Chief Taylor informed the Board that there had been a total of 14 calls for
the month of December. Medical Aids to twelve fire and 1 mutual aid.
Chief Taylor has a meeting the Chief Greenwood of Osm-is
here to go over our radios, and what the cost will be.

Chief Taylor also stated that he had spoken with Mr. McCall of Lake
Arrowhead on the possible purchase of our low bank pagers. The contract
on 12-30-87 was signed by the President of the District and sent back to Lake.
Also a letter to Lake Arrowhead Fire Protection District advising them that
we will cancel their service as of March 1, 1988.

At this time President Williams called for review and discussion on the
methods and procedures for the annual review of the general manager.
President Williams suggested: 1) change ratings to between 1 & 10, 2) it is
to be lengthy, should find a way to expedite it, 3) should be held in a
special meeting. This meeting will be February 5, 1988 at 7:30 p.m. After
discussion the rating will be as follows:
     U - Unsatisfactory         -0
     I                           0
     M                         1-3
     P                         4-6
     O                         7-10

President Williams, Chairman of the Finance Committee reported that
Security Pacific has responded, and requested additional information on how
we would repay the debt. They can't see how we could pay it back, they
have a 7 year pay back plan.

President Williams informed the Board that he and Steve Hamill of HB
Capital Resources, Ltd. are in touch on a daily basis. He is working in 5
areas. Special Districts Association are compiling a composite bond issue
for which we could qualify, however the minimum amount of money per
applicant is one million dollars.

President Williams broached the subject of Farmers Home Loan Administration
under the Community Water Sources yet, for less that 10,000 population.
Applications are accepted in any amount. It provides loaning in our case
for the life of the equipment (15 to 20 years) and is a fixed rate of
5/4%. This fluctuates, but is fixed at the time of the loan. That rate is
partially predicated on the average household income of the area. Manager
Wymer interjected that we have those figures from 1984.

Mr. Egan suggested that you might check on the funding period of FmHA, we
need the funds shortly. Mr. Egan is currently dealing with FmHA, and their
funding periods are for the next fiscal year.

President Williams brought forth discussion of the fiscal status of the
District at mid-year. We are looking to have about 30% of the revenue in
place. There are no major problems in the water dept., the only area that
would present high is in insurance, as we would anticipate. In the water
dept. we are running substantially behind both in revenue and expenditures.
The fire department is over in professional services and insurance, these
items were referred to Finance Committee. During discussion sheets will
be up-dated. Also a copy of the LAPT activity will be given to
the Board. Manager Wymer pointed out the staff problem of dividing up
interest post all the categories is difficult, he will consult with Mr.
Anderson on this.

At this time the matter of signing of the Bonds for District 3 was placed
before the Board. After discussion, Director McIlravy moved to authorize
the secretary to sign the bonds for district 3. Seconded by Director Ray.
Motion carried by a unanimous vote.

Director Medearis suggested giving the gavel to Director Bjaanes for 3
years of service. President Williams felt that this was in order and
remarked that this district is a better government agency because of Mr.
Bjaanes's presence.

Director Medearis also suggested that the agenda be changed move these
people up like Mr. Anderson so they don't have to sit here and listen to
all our problems, put them high on the list.

President Williams thanked the board for its expression of confidence.

The next regular meeting will be February 19, 1983 at 7:30 p.m.
The Special Meeting will be February 5, 1988 at 7:30 p.m.

Director Bjaanes, Chairman of the Committee for the Selection of Legal
Council informed the Board that Manager Wymer, Chief Taylor, President
Williams, Richard Tucker and himself, along with Steve Hamill had
interviewed three candidates for the position legal council for the
district. Two areas were discussed, 1) area of expertise 2) area of
general evaluation.

At the hour of 11:00 p.m. the regular meeting was adjourned to a Closed
Session for the selection of legal council.

At the hour of 11:40 p.m. the regular meeting was re-opened. Director
Bjaanes moved to terminate Richard Strong as attorney for the District
effective January 31, 1988. Seconded by Director McIlravy. Motion carried
by a unanimous vote of the Board.

Director Bjaanes moved to retain services of Joseph Aklufi of the law firm
of Aklufi & Wyocki effective February 1, 1988 under a retainer agreement
calling for a retainer of $600.00 per month for legal services. That
retainer will be in force for 3 to 6 months at the
discretion of the Board of Directors. Seconded by Director McIlravy.
Director Ray asked if the retainer of $600.00 automatically included the
expenses? After discussion

Director Bisanes amended the motion to state, the $600.00 figure is
intended to be a all inclusive figure for 8 hours of work including
expenses. There being some question as to the time and place of an
discretion of the Board at which time they will consider continuation of
his services on some other basis. Director McIlravy amended his second.
Motion carried. Director Ray voting No.

There being no further business, the President declared the meeting closed
at the hour of 11:45 p.m.

The next regular meeting will be February 19, 1988.

APPROVED:

_________________________________
Secretary


_________________________________
Baxter Williams, President

embedding.qa_text: **California Fair Political Practices Commission** March 22, 1988 William L. Corry P.O. Box 795 Running Springs, CA 92382 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. I-88-113 Dear Mr. Corry: You have asked for advice concerning the duties of the Arrowbear Park County Water District Board of Directors under the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/ The Commission's Legal Division does provide written advice to persons with questions about their duties under the Political Reform Act. (Section 83114(b).) We do not provide advice to third parties about another person's duties unless the third party has been authorized to request advice by the person whose duties are in question. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).) Therefore, we cannot provide advice on the question you have raised. However, if you believe that the Act has been violated, you may contact our Enforcement Division. That division can be reached at (916) 322-6441. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel DMG:plh 1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. William L. Conry PO 795 Running Springs, Ca. 92382 FPPC March 8, 1988 California Fair Political Practices Commission Diana M. Griffiths General Counsel 428 J street, suite 800 POBox 807 Sacramento, Ca. 95804-0807 Dear Ms Griffiths, I want to thank you for your consideration and advice on my request of Jan 26, 1988 your file I-88-035. Enclosed find copies of minutes for Arrowbear Park County Water District meetings for Dec 1987 and Jan 1988. I've underlined the portions wherein lies my concern. In December we (the public) were told that an attorney (a close friend of Director Williams) would sit in on the committee meeting, which would select those attorneys interested in representing the District. He, the attorney, would do this without charge on a friendship basis. In the next item on the agenda Director Williams made a plea for help in securing financing for the water treatment plant. He would like the firm of H.B. Capital Resources Ltd. (who he deals with in his work with the County of San Bernardino.) There were no other firms considered and the financial arrangements had already been discussed and set. The motion for hiring this firm was made by Director Williams. It passed. At the next meeting, Director Williams was elected President of the Board. He did cause the fee to be paid to H.B. Capital Resources. Later in the meeting, the attorney brought up a point that would have in part caused this fee not to be paid at this time. Later in the meeting we find that in reality, H.B. Capital Resources Ltd and Steve Harwell are one and the same. Did President Williams hand his friend $1000, and depending on funding maybe as much as $5,000? Why didn't he make it clear to the public and rest of the
